---
title: "Machine Learning Analysis on Company Bankruptcy"
description: "Comparing SVM, Random Forest, KNN and XGBoost models for bankruptcy prediction using a Taiwanese dataset."
author: "Robert Mullins"
date: 2026-04-19
image: "/images/bankruptcy.jpg"
categories: [Machine Learning, Python, Data Analytics]
---

## Overview

This project applies four machine learning models to predict company bankruptcy 
using a Taiwanese dataset from Kaggle. SMOTE oversampling and SelectKBest feature 
selection were used to address class imbalance and reduce computational cost.

::: {.callout-note}
This project was completed as part of BAA1027 Data Analytics: Machine Learning & Advanced Python at DCU.
:::

## Visualisations from Project

### Class Distribution and Shape

::: {#47cc0d8f .cell execution_count=1}
``` {.python .cell-code}
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as ticker

df = pd.read_csv("data.csv")

df.columns = df.columns.str.strip() #removes possible white space from columns that could break it

#describing the shape of the data
print("Shape of data:", df.shape) #displaying shape
print("No. of null values:", df.isnull().sum().sum()) #checking for null values
print("No. of duplicates:", df.duplicated().sum()) #checking for duplicates
print("\nClass:")
print(df["Bankrupt?" ].value_counts()) #checking class balance
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
Shape of data: (6819, 96)
No. of null values: 0
No. of duplicates: 0

Class:
Bankrupt?
0    6599
1     220
Name: count, dtype: int64
```
:::
:::


::: {#174b3216 .cell execution_count=2}
``` {.python .cell-code}
#as seen above, there is a large class imbalance
#this is why SMOTE will be needed later on to create artificial bankrupcties in data (oversampling)

counts = df["Bankrupt?"].value_counts()

plt.figure() #creating chart showing imbalanced class distribution
plt.bar(["Not Bankrupt", "Bankrupt"], [counts[0], counts[1]])
plt.ylabel("Count")
plt.title("Class Distribution")
plt.show()
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display}
